rulings.Case Title: FEI SU VS. SYNERGY PROJECT MANAGEMENT, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One, And Inspection Demand, Set One, And Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday July 2, 2015, Line 1, DEFENDANT JAVAD MIRSAIDI'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One, And Inspection Demand, Set One, And Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge William Shapiro, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing Required. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: FRANKLIN H WRIGHT VS. MARRIOTT INTERNATIONAL, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Judgmetn On The Pleadings Against Host Hotels & Resorts, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, July 2, 2015, Line 4, PLAINTIFF FRANKLIN WRIGHT'S Motion for Judgment On The Pleadings Against Host Hotels & Resorts, Inc. Denied. Defendant has filed a general denial. When ruling on a Plaintiff's motion for judgment on the pleadings, the Court must accept the allegations in the answer as true. (Sebago v. City of Alameda (1989) 211 Cal.App.3d 1372, 1381; Osborne v. Abels (1939) 30 Cal.App.2d 729, 731.) rulings.Case Title: Jones Memorial United Methodist vs. Jones Memorial Homes, etal
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Strike Defendants Memorandum Of Costs
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, July 2, 2015, Line 6, PLAINTIFF JONES MEMORIAL UNITED METHODIST CHURCH'S Motion To Strike Defendants Memorandum Of Costs. The motion is granted in part. The court strikes $963.55 from defendant's deposition costs. Defendant admits that the vendor mis-billed this amount with regard to Marvin Norman's deposition. (Bastian Dec., par. 5). The court strikes $1,689 for photocopies. See CCP 1033.5(b)(3). The remainder of the motion is denied. The remaining deposition costs are recoverable as reasonably necessary to the litigation. See CCP 1033.5(c)(2). Service on Byrde and Geter were reasonably necessary litigation costs. Lastly, defendant can recover its messenger fees under Ladas v. CSAA, (1993) 19 Cal.App.4th 761, 776. The court strikes a total of $2,652.55. rulings.Case Title: BONITA CHUN VS. NALIN MODY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendants For An Order To Transfer Venue To Napa County
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, July 2, 2015, Line 7, DEFENDANTS NALIN MODY AND MEERA MODY'S Motion For An Order To Transfer Venue To Napa County. Denied. The burden is on the moving party to make a prima facie showing that plaintiff's venue selection is not proper under any statutory grounds. (Mitchell v. Sup. Ct. (1986) 186 Cal.App.3d 1040, 1046.) Defendants have not met their initial burden because they have not submitted any admissible evidence. The Declaration of Nalin Mody and the Declaration of Meera Mody are declared under penalty of perjury under the laws of the state of Hawaii. In Kulshrestha v. First Union Comm. Corp. (2004) 33 Cal.4th 601, the Cal. Supreme Court discusses CCP sec. 2015.5, which provides that if a declaration is executed outside of California, the declaration must state that it is "declared under the laws of the State of California." The Court held that substantial compliance with the statute is insufficient, stating "[i]t seems clear that out-of-state declarations offend section 2015.5, and are not deemed sufficiently reliable for purposes of that statute, unless they follow its literal terms." (Id. at 611.) The Court concluded that an out-of-state declaration that does not explicitly state that it is executed under the laws of the State of California "cannot be used as evidence." (Id. at 618.) rulings.Case Title: SHUN LI VS. DAMIEN QUINN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Relief From Default
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, July 2, 2015, Line 9, DEFENDANTS DAMIEN QUINN, AMERICAN OPPORTUNITY PARTNERS, LLC, AND 1102 TARAVAL STREET, LLC'S Motion For Relief From Default Denied. The evidence submitted by Defendants does not overcome the presumption of correctness set forth in Evid. Code sec. 647. (See Rodriguez v. Nam Min Cho (2015) 236 Cal.App.4th 742, 750, "Evidence Code section 647 provides that a registered process server's declaration of service establishes a presumption that the facts stated in the declaration are true.") Further, the Court finds the Declaration of Javier Baraona about the service to be more credible than the Declaration of Damien Quinn. Mr. Baraona attaches contemporaneous notes that establish that Mr. Baraona got close enough to Mr. Quinn to describe his height, weight, hair color, and eye color. (Baraona Decl., Ex. A.) Mr. Baraona also declares that it is his usual practice to take notes about any unusual personal service, but he took no such notes here. (Baraona Decl., pars. 16-17.) In any event, case law holds that substantial compliance with the service requirements is sufficient to defeat a motion brought under CCP § 473(d). (Gibble v. Car-Lene Research, Inc. (1998) 67 Cal.App.4th 295, 313, "It is well settled that strict compliance with statutes governing service of process is not required. Rather, in deciding whether service was valid, the statutory provisions regarding service of process should be literally construed to effectuate service and uphold the jurisdiction of the court if actual notice has been received by the defendant;" see also Ellard v. Conway (2001) 94 Cal.App.4th 540, 544; Rutter Group, Civ. Proc. Before Trial, 5:485.) Additionally, there is insufficient evidence of mistake or excusable neglect. The Declaration of Steven Lau establishes that Mr. Quinn was granted two one-week extensions to file the answer, but did not do so. If Mr. Lau had told Mr. Quinn he would re-serve the complaint because service was defective, as contended by Mr. Quinn, there would have been no need for an extension of the time to answer. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: ROBIN WILSON VS. JUDITH A BOLTER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Defendant Judith A. Bolters Motion To Dissolve Preliminary Injunction Or For Modification Of Bond Posted By Plaintiff
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, July 2, 2015, Line 15, DEFENDANT JUDITH A. BOLTER'S Motion To Dissolve Preliminary Injunction Or For Modification Of Bond Posted By Plaintiff. Granted pursuant to CCP sec. 533. Defendants have submitted evidence establishing "a material change in the facts upon which the injunction¿was granted¿or that the ends of justice would be served by the modification or dissolution of the injunction..." Defendants submit evidence showing that two senior lienholders may take action to foreclose on the property before a final resolution is reached in this matter. Therefore, the injunction is modified as follows: Plaintiff shall post a bond in the amount of $280,000 (the amount of the principal of the loan) within 20 days of entry of this Order or the injunction will automatically dissolve. Plaintiff is not required to post a bond covering the amount of interest that Defendants seek to collect because Defendants have presented no evidence to controvert the Court's previous finding that Plaintiff has established a probability of prevailing on the merits of the Violation of the Usury Laws cause of action. Plaintiff may orally respond to Defendants' evidence introduced for the first time in reply at the hearing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: IXC HOLDINGS, INC. A DELAWARE CORPORATION VS. NETFORTRIS ACQUISITION CO., INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Netfortris Acquisition Co., Inc.S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Plaintiffs Complaint Ccp Sections 418.10 And 410.30
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, July 2, 2015, Line 16, DEFENDANT NETFORTRIS ACQUISITION CO., INC.'S Motion To Dismiss Plaintiffs Complaint CCP Sections 418.10 And 410.30. Granted. The complaint is dismissed pursuant to CCP secs. 418.10 and 410.30. (See Olinick v. BMG Entertainment (2006) 138 Cal.App.4th 1286, 1294, "The procedure for enforcing a forum selection clause is a motion to stay or dismiss for forum non conveniens.") Plaintiffs IXC Holdings, Inc., IXC, Inc., and NW, Inc. entered into an Asset Purchase Agreement with Defendant Netfortris Acquisition Co., Inc., which contains a broad forum selection clause that provides that "[t]he parties hereto hereby irrevocably submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of any federal or state court located within the City and State of New York over any dispute out of or relating to this Agreement or any of the transactions contemplated hereby and each party hereby irrevocably agrees that all claims in respect of such dispute or any suit, action, proceeding related thereto may be heard and determined in such courts." (Declaration of Grant, Ex. A, Asset Purchase Agreement, sec. 11.3.) The same parties subsequently entered into a Settlement Agreement that does not contain a forum selection clause. (Complaint, Ex. A.) Although the complaint in this action alleges breaches of the Settlement Agreement, the dispute relates to the Asset Purchase Agreement, given that the Settlement Agreement was entered into because each party alleged that the other breached the Asset Purchase Agreement. Accordingly, because the dispute set forth in the complaint relates to the Asset Purchase Agreement, the forum selection clause applies and New York is the only available venue for this dispute. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
